info.title: Workflows Executions
info.description:
## Overview
The Workflows Executions API manages the runtime instances of workflow processes within an organization.
While **Workflow Definitions** (managed by the Workflows Definitions API) serve as reusable templates
that define the structure, phases, and tasks of a process, **Workflow Executions** are the active
instances created from those definitions.

## Key Concepts

### Definitions vs Executions
- **Definition (Template)**: A blueprint defining workflow structure, phases, tasks, conditions, and automation rules
- **Execution (Instance)**: A running instance of a definition, tracking actual progress, assignees, and status

### Execution Lifecycle
1. **STARTED**: Execution is active and tasks can be worked on
2. **DONE**: All required tasks are completed
3. **CLOSED**: Execution is terminated (completed successfully or cancelled with closing reasons)

### Task Types
- **Manual Tasks**: Require human action to complete
- **Automation Tasks**: Execute configured automations automatically
- **Decision Tasks**: Evaluate conditions to determine the next path in the workflow
- **AI Agent Tasks**: Execute AI-powered agents for intelligent task processing

### Contexts
Executions are linked to entity contexts (e.g., contacts, opportunities) that provide the data
context for the workflow and allow tracking which entities a workflow operates on.

## API Versions
- **V1 (`/v1/workflows/`)**: Legacy linear phase/section/step model (deprecated for new integrations)
- **V2 (`/v2/flows/`)**: Current graph-based model with advanced features like conditional branching,
  loops, and scheduling. **Recommended for all new integrations.**

tags.name: Workflow Executions
Workflow Steps
tags.description:
Manage individual steps within V1 workflow executions (legacy). Steps represent discrete tasks
that can be assigned to users, have due dates, and track completion status. Use the Flows V2
task endpoints for new integrations.

tags.name: Closing Reasons
tags.description:
Retrieve closing reasons configured for workflow executions. When a workflow is closed/cancelled,
users can select from predefined closing reasons to document why the workflow ended. Closing
reasons are snapshots from the definition at execution creation time.

tags.name: Flows V2
tags.description:
**Recommended for new integrations.** Manage V2 flow executions using the graph-based execution model.
This API version supports advanced features including:
- Conditional branching with decision tasks
- Automation tasks with configurable triggers
- AI agent tasks for intelligent processing
- Task scheduling (immediate, delayed, or relative to events)
- Loop iterations for repeatable task sequences
- Phase-based organization with progress tracking

get.description: Retrieve Workflow Executions. Optionally, you can filter them by context & schema. Please be aware, these executions are more light weight - steps are not loaded with all information.

200.description: Success - executions loaded with success. Empty array if org has no executions.

post.description:
Creates a new V1 Workflow Execution from a workflow definition (template).

**Note:** This is the legacy V1 API. For new integrations, use `POST /v2/flows/executions` instead.

The workflow definition specifies the structure (sections and steps) of the workflow.
When created, the execution instantiates all steps and begins tracking progress.

requestBody.description: Workflow execution creation payload with definition ID and entity contexts

basicCreate.description: Create an execution linked to an opportunity entity

automatedTrigger.description: Create an execution triggered automatically by an automation rule

201.description:
Execution created successfully. Returns the complete workflow execution
including all sections, steps, and initial status.

400.description:
Validation error. Common causes include:
- Missing required workflowId
- Invalid workflowId (definition not found)
- Invalid context entity references

401.description: Authentication failed. The Bearer token is missing, expired, or invalid.

500.description: Internal server error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.description:
Retrieves a complete V1 workflow execution by ID, including all steps information.

**Note:** This is the legacy V1 API. For new integrations, use `GET /v2/flows/executions/{execution_id}` instead.

200.description: Success - execution loaded with success. Empty response execution was not found.

patch.description: Patches updates like assignees, status, closingReason for a single Workflow Execution.

requestBody.description: Patch Updates for Workflow Execution payload.

delete.description: Delete workflow execution by id. Workflow contexts will NOT be deleted.

parameters.name: executionId
parameters.description: Id of the execution to de deleted.

204.description: Success - if the execution was deleted successfully

post.description: Create a new step in current workflow execution.

201.description: Success - if the step is created with success

patch.description:
Updates a workflow execution step with new values for status, assignees, due date, position, and more.

**Note:** This is the legacy V1 API. For new integrations, use `PATCH /v2/flows/executions/{execution_id}/tasks/{task_id}` instead.

**Common use cases:**
- Mark a step as completed or skipped
- Assign or reassign users to a step
- Update step due dates (static or dynamic)
- Reorder steps within a section

parameters.name: executionId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the workflow execution

parameters.name: stepId
parameters.description: Short unique identifier (typically 6 characters) of the step within the execution

requestBody.description: Step update payload with fields to modify

markCompleted.description: Complete a step, advancing the workflow

markInProgress.description: Indicate that work has started on this step

assignUsers.description: Assign one or more users to the step

setStaticDueDate.description: Set an explicit due date for the step

setDynamicDueDate.description: Configure due date relative to workflow start or another step

reorderStep.description: Move a step to a different position within its section

200.description: Step updated successfully. Returns the updated step object with all current values.

delete.description: Deletes a step from a workflow execution.

parameters.name: stepId
parameters.description: Short uuid (length 6) to identify the Workflow Execution Step.

post.description: Search Workflow Executions by different filters.
Search workflow execution steps by different filters.

get.description:
Shows all Closing Reasons defined at the moment of starting the Workflow Execution.
The Closing Reasons shown in the execution are just snapshots
from the state of the Definition when the instance was created.

200.description: returns all Closing Reasons for this Execution

post.description:
Starts a new Flow Execution based on a flow template (definition).

The flow template defines the structure of the workflow including phases, tasks, edges (transitions),
and automation configurations. When started, the execution creates runtime instances of all tasks
and begins processing from the initial task(s).

**Required fields:**
- `flow_template_id`: The ID of the flow template to instantiate
- `contexts`: At least one entity context to link the execution to

**Optional fields:**
- `trigger`: Specifies how the execution was triggered (manual or automatic)
- `purposes`: Filter which phases/tasks are included based on taxonomy purposes

requestBody.description: Flow Execution payload containing the template ID and entity contexts

basicStart.description: Start a flow execution linked to an opportunity entity

multipleContexts.description: Start a flow execution linked to both a contact and an opportunity

withPurposes.description: Start a flow execution with specific purposes to filter applicable phases

201.description:
Flow execution started successfully. Returns the complete execution object including
all phases, tasks, edges, and initial status.

400.description:
Validation error. Common causes include:
- Missing required fields (flow_template_id, contexts)
- Invalid flow_template_id (template not found)
- Invalid entity context (entity not found)
- Empty contexts array

401.description: Authentication failed. The Bearer token is missing, expired, or invalid.

403.description:
Access forbidden. The authenticated user does not have permission to start
executions for this flow template or organization.

500.description: Internal server error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

get.description:
Retrieves a complete flow execution by ID, including all phases, tasks, edges, contexts, and analytics.

The response includes:
- **Execution metadata**: ID, name, status, timestamps, assignees
- **Phases**: Organizational groupings of tasks with progress tracking
- **Tasks**: Individual work items with their status, assignees, and configurations
- **Edges**: Connections between tasks defining the workflow graph
- **Analytics**: Timing information (started, completed, closed timestamps)
- **Contexts**: Linked entity references

patch.description: Patch flow execution with new assignees, status, analytics & other changes.

delete.description: Deletes a specific execution of a flow, identified by id. Flow contexts will NOT be deleted.

204.description: Success - if the execution was deleted successfully

post.description: Search Flow Executions for a specific Entity.

requestBody.description: Search Flow Executions for a specific Entity

patch.description:
Updates attributes of a flow task including status, assignees, due date, and more.

**Common use cases:**
- Mark a task as completed or skipped
- Assign or reassign users to a task
- Update task due dates
- Enable or disable a task
- Revert execution to a previous task

**Status transitions:**
- `PENDING` -> `IN_PROGRESS`: User starts working on the task
- `IN_PROGRESS` -> `COMPLETED`: User finishes the task
- `PENDING` or `IN_PROGRESS` -> `SKIPPED`: Task is bypassed
- `COMPLETED` or `SKIPPED` -> `PENDING`: Task is reopened (with revert_execution flag)

**Reverting execution:**
When updating a task that was already completed/skipped and comes before the current task,
use `revert_execution: true` to reset the flow back to that point. All subsequent tasks
will be reset to PENDING status.

requestBody.description: Task update payload with fields to modify

markCompleted.description: Complete a task, advancing the workflow to the next task

markInProgress.description: Indicate that work has started on this task

skipTask.description: Bypass a task without completing it

assignUsers.description: Assign one or more users to be responsible for this task

updateDueDate.description: Set an explicit due date for the task

revertToPreviousTask.description: Reset workflow back to a previously completed task, clearing all subsequent progress

decisionTaskBranch.description: For manual decision tasks, specify which condition/branch to follow

200.description:
Task updated successfully. Returns the updated task object with all current values.
If the status change triggers workflow advancement, the flow execution state is also updated.

400.description:
Validation error. Common causes include:
- Invalid status transition (e.g., COMPLETED to IN_PROGRESS without revert)
- Invalid task_id
- Invalid assigned_to user IDs
- Invalid due_date format

example.message: Invalid status transition from COMPLETED to IN_PROGRESS

401.description: Authentication failed. The Bearer token is missing, expired, or invalid.

404.description: Task or execution not found. The specified execution_id or task_id does not exist.

500.description: Internal server error. An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

post.description: Runs configured automation for a flow task

200.description: Success - when task automation has been successfully started

post.description: Executes any kind of flow task immediately.

200.description: Success - when task has been executed manually or automation has been started.

patch.description: Apply updates to a phase within flow execution

200.description: Success - when phase has been successfully updated

post.description: Create a new task in current workflow execution.

201.description: Success - if the task is created with success

delete.description: Cancels a scheduled task, deleting the schedule and marking the task as skipped.

204.description: Success - when schedule has been successfully canceled

post.description:
Reconciles an automation task's status against its linked automation execution.

Looks the task up by id (whether or not it is currently a `crt_task`),
fetches its automation execution, and — if the automation has reached a
terminal state (success/failed/skipped) — corrects the task's `status`,
`automation_config` and `analytics` to match and persists them. If the
task is the current task and the flow is still active, the flow is
advanced; otherwise only the task record is corrected.

Used by the "Refresh status" action to recover a task left `IN_PROGRESS`
after a missed or clobbered completion event. No-op (returns the
execution unchanged) when the task is already terminal or the automation
is still running.

200.description: Success - returns the (possibly updated) flow execution

post.description: Cancels the pending schedule for a task and immediately triggers its automation execution.

200.description: Success - task automation has been triggered

post.description:
Cancels a flow schedule, marking it as canceled.

**Deprecated**: Use DELETE /v2/flows/executions/{execution_id}/tasks/{task_id}/schedule instead.

204.description: Success - when schedule has been successfully canceled

ExecutionIdParam.name: execution_id
ExecutionIdParam.description:
Unique identifier for the flow execution. This ID is generated when the execution
is created and remains constant throughout its lifecycle.

TaskIdParam.name: task_id
TaskIdParam.description:
Unique identifier for a task within the execution. Each task has a unique ID
that distinguishes it from other tasks in the same execution.

PhaseIdParam.name: phase_id
PhaseIdParam.description:
Unique identifier for a phase within the execution. Phases group related tasks
together and track collective progress.

ScheduleIdParam.name: schedule_id
ScheduleIdParam.description:
Unique identifier for a scheduled task action. Schedules are created when tasks
are configured with delayed or relative timing.

SoftDeleteParam.name: soft
SoftDeleteParam.description:
When true, the execution is marked as deleted but retained in storage for archival
and audit purposes. When false (default), the execution is permanently removed.
Soft-deleted executions do not appear in normal queries.

purposes.description: An array of purposes to filter workflow phases.

closedBy.description: id of the user / partner user who is closing the workflow. For partner pass orgId_userId.

completedTime.description: Completed time of the workflow execution

creationTime.description: Creation timestamp which will double as started time as well

dueDate.description: Due date for finishing the workflow

completedTime.description: Completed time of the workflow execution

lastModifiedBy.description: Id of the user who closed workflow

enableECPWorkflow.description: Indicates whether this workflow is available for End Customer Portal or not. By default it's not.

taxonomies.description: Taxonomy ids (both Labels and Purposes) that are associated with this workflow and used for filtering

singleClosingReasonSelection.description: Indicates whether only a single closing reason can be selected when closing the workflow execution

steps.name: Send email confirming contact with the client
Call client and confirm address and product
Send email confirming contact with the client

SectionSimplified.description: A group of Steps that define the progress of the Workflow

Section.description: A group of Steps that define the progress of the Workflow

userIds.description: This field is deprecated. Please use assignedTo

taxonomies.description: Taxonomy ids (purposes in this case) that are associated with this section and used for filtering

entityRefId.description: This field is deprecated. It will be soon removed. Please use only id.
This field is deprecated. It will be soon removed. Please use only id.

partner.description: Partner-specific task details shown to partner org users viewing shared resources

userIds.description: This field is deprecated. Please use assignedTo

assignedToInProgress.description: The user which moved the step/task to the IN_PROGRESS state. The user should also be present in the assignedTo property of the step/task

assignedToOnHold.description: The user which moved the step/task to the ON_HOLD state. The user should also be present in the assignedTo property of the step/task

statusLastUpdated.description: Last updated timestamp of the status

enabled.description: enabled flag results from calculating the requirements

taxonomies.description: Taxonomy ids (purposes in this case) that are associated with this step/task and used for filtering

StepStatus.description:
**Note**: "UNASSIGNED" and "ASSIGNED" are deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Please use "PENDING" instead. Status values for workflow execution steps/tasks:
- **UNASSIGNED**: Step has not been assigned to any user (deprecated - use PENDING instead)
- **ASSIGNED**: Step has been assigned to one or more users (deprecated - use PENDING instead)
- **PENDING**: Step/Task is waiting to be started by assigned users or is ready for execution
- **IN_PROGRESS**: Step/Task is currently being worked on by a user
- **COMPLETED**: Step/Task has been finished successfully
- **SKIPPED**: Step/Task was intentionally bypassed and will not be executed
- **SCHEDULED**: Task is scheduled to run at a specific time in the future
- **CONDITION_PENDING**: Task is waiting for certain conditions to be met before it can proceed
- **WAITING_FOR_CONFIRMATION**: Step/Task is paused and waiting for user confirmation via an external input (e.g., link in email) before proceeding.
- **ON_HOLD**: Step/Task is temporarily paused and cannot proceed until manually resumed
- **FAILED**: Task encountered an error and could not be completed. Mainly for automation tasks.

complete_task_automatically.description: If true, the task be auto completed when the journey is completed

AutomationConfig.description: Configuration for automation execution to run

flowId.description: Id of the configured automation to run

executionId.description: Id of the automation execution which ran

executionStatus.description: Status of Automation Execution. Types can be found in Automation API

ECPDetails.description: Details regarding ECP for the workflow step

PartnerDetails.description: Details regarding partner for the workflow step

entityRefId.description: This field is deprecated. Please use stepId

userIds.description: This field is deprecated. Please use assignedTo

assignedToInProgress.description: The user which moved the step/task to the IN_PROGRESS state. The user should also be present in the assignedTo property of the step/task

assignedToOnHold.description: The user which moved the step/task to the ON_HOLD state. The user should also be present in the assignedTo property of the step/task

ErrorResp.description:
Standard error response returned when an API request fails.
Contains a human-readable message describing the error.

message.description: Human-readable description of the error that occurred

trigger.description:
Trigger configuration that determines when entity sync occurs.
Contains the event type and optional filter to target specific tasks/phases.

event.description:
Event or condition that triggers the entity sync.
Direct triggers match EventBridge event names (PascalCase).
Status triggers are deduced from event + entity status combination.

filter.description:
Optional filter to target specific tasks or phases.
Specify either task_template_id OR phase_template_id (mutually exclusive).
If omitted, trigger applies to all tasks/phases.

task_template_id.description: Target a specific task by its template ID (stable across executions)

phase_template_id.description: Target a specific phase by its template ID (stable across executions)

DynamicDueDate.description: set a Duedate for a step then a specific

StepRequirement.description: describe the requirement for step enablement

StartFlowReq.description: Request payload for starting a new flow execution from a template.

flow_template_id.description:
The unique identifier of the flow template (definition) to instantiate.
The template must exist and be accessible within the organization.

contexts.description:
Entity references that this execution is linked to. At least one context
is required. The primary context (is_primary: true) is used for condition
evaluation and data mapping.

purposes.description:
Taxonomy purpose IDs to filter which phases and tasks are included in the execution.
Only phases/tasks tagged with matching purposes will be active. If empty or omitted,
all phases and tasks from the template are included.

created_at.description: Creation timestamp which will double as started time as well

due_date.description: Due date for finishing the workflow

started_at.description: Timestamp when the flow execution started

completed_at.description: Timestamp when the flow execution was completed

closed_at.description: Timestamp when the flow execution was closed

started_by.description: User who started the flow execution.

closed_by.description: User who closed the flow execution

crt_since.description: Timestamp when this task entered crt_tasks (i.e. became current). Used by the flow-healing-service as the authoritative gate for "has this PENDING task been stuck long enough to heal?". Using the task's own analytics.status_updated_at as the gate produced false positives because transitioning a task INTO crt_tasks does not change its status — so that timestamp can be hours old for a freshly-current task.

_execution_chain.description: [Internal] Tracks the chain of automation-originated executions to prevent infinite loops. This is an internal property and should not be used by external consumers.

parent_execution_id.description: ID of the parent flow execution that triggered this execution via automation

parent_task_id.description: ID of the automation task in the parent execution that triggered this

depth.description: The depth in the execution chain (0 for manual triggers, 1+ for automation-triggered)

available_in_ecp.description: Indicates whether this flow execution is available for End Customer Portal or not. By default it's not.

taxonomies.description: Taxonomy ids (both Labels and Purposes) that are associated with this workflow and used for filtering

singleClosingReasonSelection.description: Indicates whether only a single closing reason can be selected when closing the flow execution

linear.description: Copied from the flow template at start. When true, task enablement is computed at runtime from the graph (a task is enabled only when all of its direct predecessor tasks in its branch are done) instead of from each task's explicit requirements.

is_primary.description: Flag to indicate if the entity is primary and should be used for evaluating the conditions of the tasks

automation_execution_id.description: ID of the automation execution, used for tracking status updates.

LoopInfo.description: Information about loop iterations, when task is part of a loop branch

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of iterations for the loop branch

crt_iterations.description: Current number of iterations for the loop branch

loop_branch_id.description: The id of the branch that will be looped

exit_branch_id.description: The id of the branch that will be used to exit the loop

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of iterations for the loop branch

crt_iterations.description: Current number of iterations for the loop branch

schedule_id.description: The id of the created schedule

scheduled_at.description:
The resolved absolute timestamp (ISO 8601, UTC) at which the task is
armed to run. Set by the backend when the schedule is armed and the
task transitions to SCHEDULED. Absent while the task is still
pending/unscheduled.

Note: intentionally typed as plain `string` (not
`format: date-time`). `schedule` is embedded in the AutomationTask /
DecisionTask schemas; if a future request body ever accepts a task
(or schedule) and `safeParse`s it, `format: date-time` would make
openapi-zod-client emit `z.string().datetime({ offset: true })` and
reject any round-tripped value that is empty or tz-less — the exact
mechanism behind the May 2026 due_date incident (513ed597 added the
format, ee574b43 activated it via an unrelated regen; see commit
2c91ff35). This field is server-written via `toISOString()` so it is
always a valid UTC instant; the datetime validator adds no
protection, only latent risk.

error_reason.description:
Set by the backend when scheduling this task FAILED (e.g. the
referenced date attribute is empty/unreadable, or the resolved fire
time is already in the past). A JSON string
({ error_code, error_reason, error_info }) describing why, so the UI
can show a specific warning and ask the user to fix the date
attribute and re-schedule. Cleared when the task is successfully
(re-)armed. This is the decision-task counterpart of
AutomationInfo.error_reason (decision tasks have no automation_config).

id.description: The id of the entity / workflow / task, based on the origin of the schedule. For all_preceding_tasks_completed, use the sentinel value 'all_preceding_tasks_completed'.

attribute.description: An entity attribute that identifies a date / datetime

schedule_id.description: The id of the created schedule

scheduled_at.description:
The resolved absolute timestamp (ISO 8601, UTC) at which the task is
armed to run. Set by the backend when the schedule is armed and the
task transitions to SCHEDULED. Absent while the task is still
pending/unscheduled.

Note: intentionally typed as plain `string` (not
`format: date-time`). `schedule` is embedded in the AutomationTask /
DecisionTask schemas; if a future request body ever accepts a task
(or schedule) and `safeParse`s it, `format: date-time` would make
openapi-zod-client emit `z.string().datetime({ offset: true })` and
reject any round-tripped value that is empty or tz-less — the exact
mechanism behind the May 2026 due_date incident (513ed597 added the
format, ee574b43 activated it via an unrelated regen; see commit
2c91ff35). This field is server-written via `toISOString()` so it is
always a valid UTC instant; the datetime validator adds no
protection, only latent risk.

error_reason.description:
Set by the backend when scheduling this task FAILED (e.g. the
referenced date attribute is empty/unreadable, or the resolved fire
time is already in the past). A JSON string
({ error_code, error_reason, error_info }) describing why, so the UI
can show a specific warning and ask the user to fix the date
attribute and re-schedule. Cleared when the task is successfully
(re-)armed. This is the decision-task counterpart of
AutomationInfo.error_reason (decision tasks have no automation_config).

allow_parallel_execution.description: When true, all branches with met conditions execute in parallel. When false, only the first branch with a met condition is executed. Defaults to true for backwards compatibility.

agent_execution_id.description: ID of the agent execution, used for tracking status updates. This is needed as a separate field to allow indexing.

AgentConfig.description: Configuration for AI Agent to run

agent_id.description: Id of the configured AI Agent to run

parameters.description: Parameters to customize the AI Agent behavior

execution_id.description: Id of the agent execution started by this task

execution_status.description: Status of the agent execution, when it already ran

outcome.description: Server-computed outcome read from the agent execution's structured_output (e.g. assigned, recommended, no_eligible_partner, missing_input). When the outcome means the work is not really done (no_eligible_partner, missing_input) the task is held as the current task instead of auto-completing, so the phase does not advance.

requirements.description: requirements that need to be fulfilled in order to enable the task while flow instances are running

created_at.description: Time when the task was created

manually_created.description: Flag to indicate if the task was created manually

enabled.description: enabled flag results from calculating the requirements

partner.description: Partner-specific task details shown to partner org users viewing shared resources

taxonomies.description: Taxonomy ids that are associated with this workflow and used for filtering
Taxonomy ids that are associated with this workflow and used for filtering

none_met.description: Indicates a default case for a decision task. Only decision task edges can have this field and the flow advances using this edge if no conditions are met.

flow_id.description: Id of the automation that should be run by this task

execution_id.description: Id of the automation execution, when it already ran

execution_status.description: Status of the automation execution, when it already ran

heal_attempts.description: Internal — number of times flow-healing-service has attempted to re-trigger this task's lost automation dispatch. Used to cap retries and avoid an indefinite heal-on-every-read storm against a deterministically-failing automation flow.

last_heal_attempted_at.description: Internal — timestamp of the most recent heal attempt for this task. flow-healing-service uses this as a per-task debounce gate so the heal cannot fire more than once per HEAL_RETRY_COOLDOWN_MS regardless of how often the flow execution is read.

AutomationInputContext.description: Optional. Source of the entity fed into this automation task. If omitted, the workflow's primary entity is used.

source.description: `trigger` = workflow's primary (trigger) entity. `task` = entity produced by an upstream task in the graph.

task_id.description: Required when source is `task`. The id of the upstream task whose output entity should feed this task.

status_updated_at.description: Last updated timestamp of the status

in_progress_by.description: The user which moved the task/phase to IN_PROGRESS state.

completed_by.description: The user which moved the task/phase to COMPLETED state.

skipped_by.description: The user which moved the task/phase to SKIPPED state.

on_hold_by.description: The user which moved the task/phase to ON_HOLD state.

ConditionId.description: A locally unique identifier for the condition

evaluated_at.description: Time when the condition was evaluated

is_met.description: The result of evaluating this condition - true / false

value_type.description: How to interpret values. "static" (default) means literal values. "relative_date" means values[0] is a dynamic date token like "today".

id.description: The id of the action or trigger

attributes.description:
Multi-attribute mode. When present and length > 1, the statement is
evaluated against every listed attribute and combined via
`attributes_match`. All listed attributes must share the same
`attribute_type`. Mutually exclusive with `attribute_sub_field`,
`date_offset`, and `attribute_operation`. When absent or length === 1,
the legacy `attribute` field is used.

attributes_match.description:
Inner connector across `attributes`. `any` (default) means at least
one attribute must satisfy the operator; `all` means every attribute
must satisfy it. Ignored when `attributes` is absent or has length < 2.

attribute_sub_field.description: For complex attribute types, specifies which sub-field to extract (e.g., 'address', 'name', 'email_type')

date_offset.description: Offset to apply to the source date value before comparison (e.g., +18 years for age check, +30 days for expiry)

DueDateConfig.description: Set due date for the task based on a dynamic condition

EnableRequirement.description: describe the requirement for a task to be enabled

task_id.description: The template_id of the task that this requirement points to

phase_id.description: The template_id of the phase that this requirement points to

VariableAssignment.description: Represents a variable assignment with its expression and optional resolved value. Used for dynamic user assignments that get resolved during workflow execution.

value.description: The resolved values after variable evaluation (populated during execution)

Assignees.description: The user ids or variable assignments

PatchTaskReq.description:
Request payload for updating a task within a flow execution.
All fields are optional; only provided fields will be updated.

due_date.description:
Explicit due date for the task. Takes precedence over
due_date_config if both are provided.

Note: intentionally typed as plain `string` (not
`format: date-time`). For day/week/month-precision due
dates the server stores a "floating" datetime without a
timezone designator (e.g. `2026-05-28T00:00:00.000`) so
that the UI can render it as a date in the user's local
timezone without shifting the displayed day. Tightening
this to `format: date-time` causes openapi-zod-client to
emit `z.string().datetime({ offset: true })` in
`validators-generated.ts`, which then trips
`safeParse(body)` in `patch-task.ts` whenever the
sidebar sends a stored task back with a tz-less
`due_date`. See commit 4aca299c (Aug 2024) for the
original date-only display rationale and the May 2026
incident (513ed597 added the format, ee574b43
unintentionally activated it via an unrelated regen) for
the history. Long-term, day-precision due dates should
migrate to a separate `format: date` field.

enabled.description:
Controls whether the task is enabled (can be worked on) or disabled (grayed out).
Disabled tasks cannot have their status changed until re-enabled.

partner.description: Partner-specific task details shown to partner org users viewing shared resources

next_condition_id.description:
For decision tasks with manual trigger mode, specifies which condition/branch
to follow when completing the task. The condition ID must match one of the
conditions defined on the decision task.

revert_execution.description:
Controls behavior when updating a task that was already completed/skipped and
comes before the current task in the workflow:
- `true`: Reverts the execution - the patched task becomes the current task
  and all subsequent tasks are reset to PENDING status
- `false` (default): Updates only this task without affecting workflow position
  or other tasks

**Important:** This parameter is silently ignored when:
- Patching the current task
- Patching future tasks (tasks that haven't been reached yet)

completed_via_journey.description:
Request-only signal indicating the task is being completed as a
result of the user submitting the task's linked journey (journey
auto-completion). When `true` and the task transitions to
`COMPLETED`, the activity log records a journey-specific message
instead of the generic completion message. Not persisted on the task.

previous_task_id.description: Source node id for the edge to this task

next_task_id.description: Target node id for the edge from this task

enabled.description: flag for controlling enabled/disabled state of the task
